summary: God-file entry point: CLI flag parsing, dual-mode dispatch (TUI vs channel-fetch), service wiring, and all adapter/helper types

requires: YAML config file (default: config.yaml) with telegram.api_id, api_hash, session_file, storage.db_path
--channel flag required unless --tui flag is set
SQLite database at cfg.Storage.DBPath (created by storage.Open)
Telegram API credentials valid and reachable for authentication
guarantees: Graceful shutdown on SIGINT via signal.NotifyContext
store.Close() called on exit via defer
Fatalf on any startup failure — never partial initialization

effects: Telegram API network calls (authentication, channel resolution, message fetch)
Ollama HTTP health check at startup if provider=ollama
OpenRouter HTTP calls via summarizer if summarization requested
Terminal I/O (stdout message printing, TUI screen)
state: Stateless at package level; all runtime state held by store, client, and tui.App. registryRefreshAdapter and summarizeServiceAdapter are thin, stateless wrappers.

observations.text: Two distinct modes: TUI mode (--tui) launches bubbletea full-screen app with auto-refresh and summarization; CLI mode (--channel) does a one-shot fetch+store+optional-summarize
storeMessages inserts messages one-by-one in a loop with no batch operation — O(N) round-trips to SQLite for each fetch
ensureSourceChannel silently swallows DB errors (log.Printf only) — if channel creation fails, the source will have no DB record, but the TUI will proceed anyway
The --summarize flag accepts N days but the value is never used: SummarizeUnsummarized processes all unsummarized messages regardless of the N value

Bidirectional coupling with tui package: main.go passes store+opts to tui.NewApp, and implements tui.RefreshService and tui.SummarizeService interfaces (registryRefreshAdapter, summarizeServiceAdapter) to call back into refresh/summarizer packages
buildLLMClient performs a live Ollama health check and model availability check at startup — will block and fail-fast if Ollama is unreachable or model is missing
registryRefreshAdapter does in-memory name-set filtering of sources for RefreshFiltered — the filtering is O(N) against the full source list per call
storeChannel uses the channel username as the Title field with a comment 'We could enhance this by fetching actual title' — display names in the TUI will show raw usernames like @durov
updateSyncState performs a linear scan over all fetched messages to find the maximum ID rather than relying on ordering; correct but redundant if messages arrive in order
